intro: Rubus raduloides, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan strawberry, is a species of bramble that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rubus raduloides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
